title: Number Theory Deep: Dirichlet L-Functions, Primes & Zeta Zeros

key_theorem.statement: The von Mangoldt explicit formula decomposes the prime-counting function ψ(x) (EML-∞: irregular step function) into EML-1 (main term x) + EML-3 oscillations (Σ x^ρ/ρ with ρ on critical line). This is the EML depth decomposition of primes: EML-∞ = EML-1 + EML-3. Dirichlet L-functions are EML-3 (Euler product over EML-3 characters). Möbius M(x) is EML-∞ unconditionally; EML-3 under RH.

dirichlet_L_functions.eml_reason: χ(n) periodic + n^{-s} = exp(-s·ln n) → EML-3 series

characters_mod4.name: χ₀ (principal)
characters_mod4.reason: Principal character: χ₀(n)=1 if gcd(n,q)=1 else 0 — EML-0 (integer-valued)

characters_mod4.name: χ₁ (Kronecker symbol mod 4)
characters_mod4.reason: Values in {-1,0,1} — EML-0 per value; pattern = EML-3 (periodic = exp(2πin/4))

dirichlet_L_functions.GRH: Generalized RH: zeros of L(s,χ) on Re(s)=1/2 — EML-3 critical line

explicit_formula.formula: ψ(x) = x - Σ_ρ x^ρ/ρ - ln(2π) - ½ln(1-x^{-2})
explicit_formula.depth_decomposition: EML-∞ prime staircase = EML-1 main + EML-3 zeros — the explicit formula is an EML depth decomposition

mobius_mu.reason_under_rh: M(x) = O(x^{1/2+ε}) under RH: x^{1/2} = EML-3

mertens_conjecture.reason: Disproof uses EML-∞ oscillations in M(x) that exceed √x

eml_depth_summary.EML-0: μ(n) per value ∈ {-1,0,1}; λ(n) per value; prime indicators
eml_depth_summary.EML-1: Main term x in explicit formula; prime number theorem x/ln(x)
eml_depth_summary.EML-2: ln(x) corrections; Meissel-Mertens constant; prime gap mean ln(p)
eml_depth_summary.EML-3: x^{1/2+iγ} oscillatory terms; L(s,χ) Dirichlet series; M(x) under RH
eml_depth_summary.EML-\u221e: ψ(x) full prime staircase; M(x) unconditional; prime gap max

rabbit_hole_log: The explicit formula is literally an EML decomposition: it writes an EML-∞ object as EML-1 + (sum of EML-3 terms). Each Riemann zero contributes one EML-3 frequency. RH = all frequencies have amplitude x^{1/2} (EML-3 bound).
Dirichlet characters χ(n) mod q: their Gauss sums τ(χ) = Σ χ(n)exp(2πin/q) are EML-3 (exponential sum). |τ(χ)|² = q for primitive χ — EML-0 = q (integer).
Twin prime conjecture: prime gaps p_{n+1}-p_n are EML-∞ (no formula). Hardy-Littlewood conjectures: density of twin primes ~ 2C₂·x/ln²x (EML-2 density).

connections.to_session_89: Session 89: RH zeros = EML-3. Session 90: explicit formula uses those zeros to reconstruct EML-∞ prime distribution
connections.to_session_87: Session 87: Dedekind η(τ) = EML-3. Session 90: L(s,χ) = EML-3 — both are EML-3 arithmetic series
